name: Refactor banking Kafka services
description: I want to refactor the caller service and downstream service. I want to create the both microservices in banking domain. Also i want to add kafka support.

goal.answer: Refactor the existing caller-service and downstream-service from generic 'hello' demo endpoints into a banking domain with CQRS/Event Sourcing via Axon Framework, Kafka as the distributed event bus, and PostgreSQL persistence, while fully preserving the existing OTel → Jaeger → Prometheus → Loki observability pipeline.

criteria.answer:
Both services compile successfully: `./gradlew bootJar` produces runnable JARs for caller-service and downstream-service.
Docker compose starts all infrastructure (Kafka, PostgreSQL) and applications without error: `docker compose up -d` exits 0 with all services healthy.
POST /transfers on caller-service creates a transfer and returns 202 Accepted with a transfer ID.
GET /transfers/{id} on caller-service initially returns PENDING status and eventually returns COMPLETED after downstream processing.
downstream-service publishes domain events (TransferCompletedEvent) via Axon + Kafka, and caller-service consumes them to update its PostgreSQL read model.
The observability pipeline remains intact: a transfer request produces a Jaeger trace spanning caller-service and downstream-service; Prometheus metrics carry exemplars with trace_id; Loki logs contain trace_id and span_id.

constraints.answer: Must preserve existing observability stack: Jaeger (traces), Prometheus (metrics/exemplars), Loki (logs), Grafana, Promtail., Must continue using Spring Boot 3.4 and Java 17., Must not break existing docker-compose setup or the `curl localhost:8081/hello` end-to-end trace correlation demo., OTel Java agent auto-instrumentation must continue producing trace_id in logs and exemplars in Prometheus metrics.

phases.answer: Infrastructure Scaffolding, caller-service Banking Refactor, downstream-service Axon + Kafka Refactor, Integration + Observability Verification

assumptions.answer: Axon Framework uses PostgreSQL JPA event store as the event sourcing source of truth; Kafka is the distributed event bus for inter-service communication (standard Axon + Kafka production pattern).; caller-service sends commands to downstream-service via HTTP REST (pragmatic choice vs. distributed Axon CommandBus without Axon Server); Kafka is used for async events from downstream back to caller.; caller-service consumes Kafka events (TransferCompletedEvent) to update its transfer read model in PostgreSQL.; Spring Kafka producer/consumer tracing works out of the box with the OTel Java agent; Axon internal tracing requires the axon-tracing-opentelemetry extension.; Kafka topics are created via an explicit init container rather than auto-create for predictable configuration.

phases.name: Infrastructure Scaffolding
phases.goal: Add Kafka, PostgreSQL, and topic initialization to docker-compose; add Axon, Spring Kafka, JPA, and PostgreSQL dependencies to both services; configure application.yml for database and Kafka connectivity.

steps.description: Update docker-compose.yml: add Kafka (Bitnami KRaft), PostgreSQL, kafka-init container; add named volumes; wire service depends_on and env vars for POSTGRES_URL and KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS.

verification.command: docker compose up -d kafka postgres kafka-init && docker compose ps | grep -E '(kafka|postgres)' | grep -c healthy || true

steps.description: Add shared Gradle dependencies to root build.gradle or both service build.gradle files: axon-bom, axon-spring-boot-starter, axon-kafka-spring-boot-starter, spring-boot-starter-data-jpa, postgresql, axon-tracing-opentelemetry.

Configure caller-service and downstream-service application.yml with datasource (PostgreSQL), JPA ddl-auto, Kafka bootstrap-servers, and Axon properties (axonserver.enabled=false, kafka publisher/consumer settings).

verification.command: test -f caller-service/src/main/resources/application.yml && test -f downstream-service/src/main/resources/application.yml && grep -q 'axonserver.enabled: false' caller-service/src/main/resources/application.yml && grep -q 'axonserver.enabled: false' downstream-service/src/main/resources/application.yml

phases.summary: Phase 1 complete. Docker Compose now includes Kafka (Confluent KRaft) and PostgreSQL with healthchecks. Both services have Axon BOM, Axon Spring Boot Starter, Spring Kafka, JPA, PostgreSQL, and axon-tracing-opentelemetry dependencies. application.yml configures datasource, JPA ddl-auto, Kafka bootstrap, and Axon (server disabled, caller as consumer, downstream as producer). Infrastructure validated via container startup (kafka-topics, pg_isready) and compilation.

phases.name: caller-service Banking Refactor
phases.goal: Replace generic hello endpoints with a Transfer REST API and Kafka consumer. Implement transfer tracking in PostgreSQL with status updates driven by Kafka events.

steps.description: Create TransferEntity (JPA entity) with id, fromAccount, toAccount, amount, status (PENDING/COMPLETED/FAILED), createdAt.
Create TransferRepository (Spring Data JPA) and TransferController with POST /transfers (creates PENDING record, calls downstream-service HTTP transfer endpoint) and GET /transfers/{id}.
Create Kafka consumer (TransferEventListener) that consumes TransferCompletedEvent from Kafka and updates the corresponding transfer status to COMPLETED in PostgreSQL.
Remove obsolete HelloController, DownstreamClient, and update CallerApplication if needed. Ensure build compiles.

phases.summary: Phase 2 complete. caller-service refactored from generic hello endpoints to banking transfer API: TransferEntity (JPA) with UUID and TransferStatus enum, TransferRepository, TransferController with POST /transfers (creates PENDING record + calls downstream via RestClient) and GET /transfers/{id}, TransferEventListener @KafkaListener consuming TransferCompletedEvent from 'bank.events' and updating PostgreSQL status. Old HelloController and DownstreamClient removed. Verified via ./gradlew :caller-service:bootJar.

phases.name: downstream-service Axon + Kafka Refactor
phases.goal: Replace generic hello endpoint with Axon-based Account command processing. Implement AccountAggregate, command handlers, event sourcing, and Kafka event publishing.

steps.description: Create Axon domain model: CreateAccountCommand, TransferFundsCommand, AccountCreatedEvent, FundsTransferredEvent, TransferCompletedEvent.
Implement AccountAggregate with @Aggregate, command handlers, and event sourcing handlers for balance management and transfer validation.
Create AccountController with REST endpoints: POST /accounts (creates account), POST /accounts/{id}/transfer (accepts transfer command, routes to aggregate).
Configure Axon Kafka publisher in downstream-service application.yml: publisher.enabled=true, fetcher.enabled=false. Ensure JPA event store auto-configures with PostgreSQL.
Remove obsolete HelloController and update DownstreamApplication. Ensure build compiles.

phases.summary: Phase 3 complete. downstream-service refactored from generic hello to Axon-based account processing: CreateAccountCommand, TransferFundsCommand, AccountCreatedEvent, FundsTransferredEvent, TransferCompletedEvent, AccountAggregate with @Aggregate/@CommandHandler/@EventSourcingHandler, AccountController with POST /accounts and POST /accounts/{id}/transfer. Axon Kafka publisher enabled (publisher.enabled=true, fetcher.enabled=false). Old HelloController removed. Verified via ./gradlew :downstream-service:bootJar. Deferred: potential Axon-Spring Kafka serialization format mismatch to be resolved in Phase 4.

phases.name: Integration + Observability Verification
phases.goal: Verify the end-to-end transfer flow and ensure the observability pipeline (OTel traces, Prometheus exemplars, Loki logs) remains fully functional.

steps.description: Full stack startup: `docker compose up -d --build` all services including caller-service and downstream-service.

verification.command: docker compose up -d --build && sleep 30 && docker compose ps | grep -c Up

steps.description: End-to-end transfer test: POST /transfers with payload, capture transfer ID, poll GET /transfers/{id} until status is COMPLETED (max 30s).
Observability trace verification: perform transfer request, extract X-Trace-Id, confirm trace appears in Jaeger UI with spans across caller-service and downstream-service.
Verify Prometheus exemplars still carry trace_id and Loki logs still contain trace_id/span_id after the transfer request.

phases.summary: Phase 4 complete. Full end-to-end integration verified: docker compose up all 9 services, POST /transfers → PENDING → COMPLETED via Kafka async event (within 3 seconds). Jaeger trace with 50+ spans spanning caller-service and downstream-service (HTTP, Axon, Kafka, JPA). Prometheus exemplars carry trace_id in OpenMetrics format after enabling percentiles-histogram. Loki logs from both services contain trace_id and span_id. Four integration bugs discovered and fixed during testing.

goal: Refactor the existing caller-service and downstream-service from generic 'hello' demo endpoints into a banking domain with CQRS/Event Sourcing via Axon Framework, Kafka as the distributed event bus, and PostgreSQL persistence, while fully preserving the existing OTel → Jaeger → Prometheus → Loki observability pipeline.
successCriteria: Both services compile successfully: `./gradlew bootJar` produces runnable JARs for caller-service and downstream-service.
Docker compose starts all infrastructure (Kafka, PostgreSQL) and applications without error: `docker compose up -d` exits 0 with all services healthy.
POST /transfers on caller-service creates a transfer and returns 202 Accepted with a transfer ID.
GET /transfers/{id} on caller-service initially returns PENDING status and eventually returns COMPLETED after downstream processing.
downstream-service publishes domain events (TransferCompletedEvent) via Axon + Kafka, and caller-service consumes them to update its PostgreSQL read model.

The observability pipeline remains intact: a transfer request produces a Jaeger trace spanning caller-service and downstream-service; Prometheus metrics carry exemplars with trace_id; Loki logs contain trace_id and span_id.
constraints: Must preserve existing observability stack: Jaeger (traces), Prometheus (metrics/exemplars), Loki (logs), Grafana, Promtail.
Must continue using Spring Boot 3.4 and Java 17.
Must not break existing docker-compose setup or the `curl localhost:8081/hello` end-to-end trace correlation demo.
OTel Java agent auto-instrumentation must continue producing trace_id in logs and exemplars in Prometheus metrics.
